perturbation.original_text: During the Term, Programmer will obtain and maintain in full force and effect in its own name all necessary or appropriate music licenses with respect to the WLIB Programs rebroadcast on the HD2 Channel. Programmer represents and warrants to Licensee that Programmer has all rights in and to the WLIB Programs necessary or appropriate to rebroadcast such WLIB Programs on the HD2 Channel.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term, Programmer will obtain and maintain in full force and effect in its own name all necessary or appropriate music licenses with respect to the WLIB Programs rebroadcast on the HD2 Channel. The Broadcaster represents and warrants to Licensee that Programmer has all rights in and to the WLIB Programs necessary or appropriate to rebroadcast such WLIB Programs on the HD2 Channel.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'Programmer' to 'The Broadcaster' introduces confusion regarding who is responsible for the warranties. 'Programmer' is defined earlier in the agreement, while 'The Broadcaster' is not. This contradiction creates uncertainty regarding which entity is actually making the warranties, as the document becomes internally inconsistent. This creates an In-Text Contradiction, by using the term 'Broadcaster' when the contract does not define this term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Term, Programmer will obtain and maintain in full force and effect in its own name all necessary or appropriate music licenses with respect to the WLIB Programs rebroadcast on the HD2 Channel. The Broadcaster represents and warrants to Licensee that Programmer has all rights in and to the WLIB Programs necessary or appropriate to rebroadcast such WLIB Programs on the HD2 Channel.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Programmer' with 'The Broadcaster' when referring to who represents and warrants to the Licensee that 'Programmer' has all rights. Since 'The Broadcaster' is undefined, this creates a contradiction because the document becomes unclear about which entity is making the warranties. This directly contradicts the original intent where 'Programmer,' a defined term, was responsible for these warranties, creating confusion and potential unenforceability. This falls under the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, as it uses an undefined term ('The Broadcaster') where a defined term ('Programmer') was previously used, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Programmer shall indemnify and hold Licensee harmless against any and all liability arising from Programmer's use of Licensee's facilities, if any, or from the broadcast of the WLIB Programs on the HD2 Channel, including without limitation for libel, slander, illegal competition or trade practice, infringement of trademarks, trade names, or program titles, violation of rights of privacy, and infringement of copyrights and proprietary rights or any other violation of third party rights or FCC rules or other applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: Programmer shall indemnify and hold Licensee harmless against any and all liability arising from WLIB's use of Licensee's facilities, if any, or from the broadcast of the WLIB Programs on the HD2 Channel, including without limitation for libel, slander, illegal competition or trade practice, infringement of trademarks, trade names, or program titles, violation of rights of privacy, and infringement of copyrights and proprietary rights or any other violation of third party rights or FCC rules or other applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Programmer's use' with 'WLIB's use' introduces a contradiction. While WLIB Programs are mentioned, WLIB itself is defined as radio broadcast station (WLIB-AM, New York, NY). It isn't clarified how a radio station can 'use Licensee's facilities'. This change contradicts the understanding that the Programmer is the entity responsible for the programming and its actions. This creates an In-Text Contradiction, by using the term 'WLIB's use of Licensee's facilities', when the contract does not define this how the liability is being shared by WLIB vs Programmer.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Programmer shall indemnify and hold Licensee harmless against any and all liability arising from WLIB's use of Licensee's facilities, if any, or from the broadcast of the WLIB Programs on the HD2 Channel, including without limitation for libel, slander, illegal competition or trade practice, infringement of trademarks, trade names, or program titles, violation of rights of privacy, and infringement of copyrights and proprietary rights or any other violation of third party rights or FCC rules or other applicable law.
perturbation.justification: While the change from 'Programmer's use' to 'WLIB's use' introduces ambiguity, it doesn't create a direct and undeniable contradiction. Both the changed and contradicted text are identical. Therefore, the change creates potential uncertainty about liability, the text is still essentially the same and doesn't invalidate or break compatibility with anything else in the document. The contradiction isn't strong enough for a definite 'YES' per the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: During the Term, Licensee will retain all rights to the call letters of the Station or any other call letters which may be assigned by the FCC for use by the Station. Programmer shall include in the WLIB Programs it delivers for broadcast an announcement at the beginning of each hour of such WLIB Programs to identify such call letters, as well as any other announcements required by the rules and regulations of the FCC. Programmer is specifically authorized to use such call letters in its WLIB Programs and in any promotional material, in any media, used to promote the WLIB Programs.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term, Licensee will retain all rights to the call letters of the Channel or any other call letters which may be assigned by the FCC for use by the Station. Programmer shall include in the WLIB Programs it delivers for broadcast an announcement at the beginning of each hour of such WLIB Programs to identify such call letters, as well as any other announcements required by the rules and regulations of the FCC. Programmer is specifically authorized to use such call letters in its WLIB Programs and in any promotional material, in any media, used to promote the WLIB Programs.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Station' to 'Channel' makes it ambiguous which entity owns the right to the call letters. The 'Station' is clearly defined at the beginning of the contract, while 'Channel' is not as clearly defined. This introduces uncertainty as to whether the rights to the call letters belong to the overall broadcasting station or specifically to the HD2 Channel which this agreement is centered on. This creates an In-Text Contradiction, by creating a doubt between whom has the right to the letters since it starts talking about 'Station' and 'Channel'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Term, Licensee will retain all rights to the call letters of the Channel or any other call letters which may be assigned by the FCC for use by the Station. Programmer shall include in the WLIB Programs it delivers for broadcast an announcement at the beginning of each hour of such WLIB Programs to identify such call letters, as well as any other announcements required by the rules and regulations of the FCC. Programmer is specifically authorized to use such call letters in its WLIB Programs and in any promotional material, in any media, used to promote the WLIB Programs.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Station' to 'Channel' introduces ambiguity regarding the ownership of the call letters. The original text clearly assigns these rights based on the 'Station', which is a defined term. The changed text introduces 'Channel', which is not clearly defined in relation to 'Station', potentially conflicting with the intention of granting call letter rights to the overall broadcasting entity. Since the Changed Text is the same as the Contradicted Text, the contradiction exists in the same place. This adheres to the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the contract uses multiple terms (Station and Channel) leading to conflicting obligations and confusion.
